name: adapter
description: Sets the default Video Adapter.
since:
Sets the default Video Adapter.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -adapter=MISSING_AN_EXAMPLE

name: admins
description: Whitelisted clients can use #login without password. See .

name: allowedHTMLLoadExtensions
description: Only allow files and URLs with listed extensions to be loaded via htmlLoad command. Not listing any extension means everything is allowed. Defining the setting as empty arrays means nothing is allowed.

name: allowedHTMLLoadURIs
description: Only allow files from listed URIs and URLs to be loaded via htmlLoad command. Comment out if not used. Can use += to add to the existing list. Not listing any extension means everything is allowed. Defining the setting as empty arrays means nothing is allowed.

name: allowedLoadFileExtensions
description: Only allow files with listed extensions to be loaded via loadFile command. Not listing any extension means everything is allowed. Defining the setting as empty arrays means nothing is allowed.

name: allowedPreprocessFileExtensions
description: Only allow files with listed extensions to be loaded via preprocessFile / preprocessFileLineNumbers commands. Not listing any extension means everything is allowed. Defining the setting as empty arrays means nothing is allowed.

name: allowedVoteCmds
description: See Arma 3: Mission voting.

name: allowedVotedAdminCmds
description: See Arma 3: Mission voting.

name: autoInit
description: Automatically initialize mission just like the first client does.
since:
Automatically initialize mission just like the first client does.

The server config file (server.cfg) must contain Persistent = 1; otherwise the  parameter is skipped.



; Example
 arma3server_x64.exe -autoInit

name: autoReport
description: (former autoSpot) Automatic reporting of spotted enemied by players only. This doesn't have any effect on AIs.

name: autoSelectMission
description: When enabled, the server auto-starts next mission in mission cycle and waits for players in the role selection. This allows full mission information in server browser and then results in proper filtering of the servers. This is lesser-variant (trimmed) of server startup command-line parameter -autoInit. Might collide with campaign linked missions / need mission cycle etc.

name: autotest
description: Loads automatically a series of defined missions and on error writes to a log file.

name: bandwidthAlg
description: Set to 2 to use a new experimental networking algorithm that might be better than the default one.

name: BattlEye
description: Enables or disables the BattlEye anti-cheat engine. Requires installed BattlEye on server and clients joining the server

name: bePath
description: By default BattlEye will create BattlEye folder inside server profile folder. With -bePath param it is possible to specify a custom folder.
since:
By default BattlEye will create BattlEye folder inside server profile folder. With -bePath param it is possible to specify a custom folder.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -bePath=C:\MyBattlEyeFolder

name: beta
description: Loads the specified beta sub-folders. This allows use of beta build w/o disabling in-game mod/extension management (in UI menu).

name: briefingTimeOut
description: Briefing timeout

name: buldozer
description: Starts Buldozer mode.
since:
Starts Buldozer mode.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -buldozer

name: callExtReportLimit
description: If server initiated callExtension takes longer than specified limit in milliseconds, the warning will be logged into server .rpt file as well as reflected in the extension return result.

name: cameraShake
description: Camera shake

name: cfg
description: Selects the Server Basic Config file. Config file for server specific settings like network performance tuning.

name: checkSignatures
description: Provide a thorough test of all signatures of all loaded banks (PBOs) at the start of the game. Only the stored sha1 values are verified with signatures/keys. Output is in  file.
since:
Provide a thorough test of all signatures of all loaded banks (PBOs) at the start of the game. Only the stored sha1 values are verified with signatures/keys. Output is in  file.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -checkSignatures

name: checkSignaturesFull
description: Same as above, but checks every byte of the file content, and therefore not only verifies signatures, but also verifies file integrity.
since:
Same as above, but checks every byte of the file content, and therefore not only verifies signatures, but also verifies file integrity.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -checkSignaturesFull

name: client
description: Launch as client (console). Useful for headless clients.
since:
Launch as client (console). Useful for headless clients.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -client

name: command
description:
Creates  "\\.\pipe\name" for receiving predefined set of commands. See Arma 3: Named Pipe.
Note that it works only on clients, but not on dedicated server.

name: commands
description: Commands (0 = never, 1 = fade out, 2 = always)

name: config
description: Selects the Server Config File. Config file for server specific settings like admin password and mission selection.
since:
Selects the Server Config File. Config file for server specific settings like admin password and mission selection.

; Example
 arma3server_x64.exe "-config=C:\My Server\config.cfg"

name: connect
description: Server IP to connect to.
since:
Server IP to connect to.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -connect=168.152.15.147

name: cpuCount
description: Change to a number less or equal than numbers of available cores. This will override auto detection (which equate to native cores).

name: crashDiag
description: Extra crash-logging in performance binary, added on v18 (https://forums.bistudio.com/topic/160288-arma-3-stable-server-154-performance-binary-feedback/?p=2979101)
since:
Extra crash-logging in performance binary, added on v18 (https://forums.bistudio.com/topic/160288-arma-3-stable-server-154-performance-binary-feedback/?p=2979101)

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -crashDiag
-->

name: d3dNoLock
description: Doesn't lock the VRAM.
since:
Doesn't lock the VRAM.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -d3dNoLock

name: d3dNoMultiCB
description: D3D uses Single Constant Buffers instead of Multiple Constant Buffers.
since:
D3D uses Single Constant Buffers instead of Multiple Constant Buffers.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -d3dNoMultiCB

<!--

name: deathMessages
description: Killed by

name: debriefingTimeOut
description: Debriefing timeout

name: debug
description: Enables more verbose error logging. See Arma 3: Debug Mode
since:
Enables more verbose error logging. See Arma 3: Debug Mode

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -debug

name: debugCallExtension
description: Logs extension calls in the rpt log like this: File:arma3-startup_parameter_debugCallExtension_output.png
since:
Logs extension calls in the rpt log like this: File:arma3-startup_parameter_debugCallExtension_output.png

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -debugCallExtension

name: detectedMines
description: Detected mines (0 = never, 1 = limited distance, 2 = always)

name: disableServerThread
description: Option to disable the server send messaging thread in case of random crashing (may also decrease performance of server on multicore CPUs)
since:
Option to disable the server send messaging thread in case of random crashing (may also decrease performance of server on multicore CPUs)

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -disableServerThread

name: disableVoN
description: Enables or disables the ''Voice over Net'' (0 is VoN enabled (default), 1 is disabled).

name: DisconnectTimeout
description: Server wait time before disconnecting client after loss of active traffic connection, range 1 to 90 seconds.

name: doNothing
description: Engine closes immediately after detecting this option.
since:
Engine closes immediately after detecting this option.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -doNothing

name: doubleIdDetected
description: rowspan="8" align="center" | See Server Side Scripting

name: drawingInMap
description: Enables or disables the ability to place markers and draw lines in map.

name: enableHT
description:
Enables the use of all logical CPU cores for parallel tasks processing. If the CPU does not support Hyper-Threading or similar technology, this parameter is ignored.
When disabled, only physical cores are used.

name: enablePlayerDiag
description: Logs players' bandwidth and desync info every 60 seconds, as well as "network message is pending" owner identity.

name: enemyTags
description: Enemy name tags (0 = never, 1 = limited distance, 2 = always)

name: exThreads
description: Change to a number 0,1,3,5,7. This will override auto detection (which use 3 for dualcore and 7 for quadcore).
since:
Change to a number 0,1,3,5,7. This will override auto detection (which use 3 for dualcore and 7 for quadcore).

All file operations go through a dedicated thread. This offloads some processing from the main thread, however it adds some overhead at the same time.

The reason why threaded file ops were implemented was to serve as a basement for other threads ops. When multiple threads are running at the same time, OS is scheduling them on different cores.
Geometry and Texture loading (both done by the same thread) are scheduled on different cores outside the main rendering loop at the same time with the main rendering loop.

{| class="wikitable sortable valign-top align-center"
|+ Ex(tra)threads table
! Parameter
! class="unsortable" colspan="3" | Description
|-
! Number !! Geometry loading !! Texture loading !! File operations
|-
! 0
|  ||  || 
|-
! 1
|  ||  || 
|-
! 3
|  ||  || 
|-
! 5
|  ||  || 
|-
! 7
|  ||  || 
|}

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -exThreads=7

name: filePatchingExceptions
description: Whitelisted clients ignore the rules defined by allowedFilePatching and verifySignatures, allowing them to join the server with any mods of their choice. Signature errors are still logged by the server upon connection.

name: forceRotorLibSimulation
description: Enforces the Advanced Flight Model on the server. 0 (up to the player). 1 - forced AFM, 2 - forced SFM.

name: friendlyTags
description: Friendly name tags (0 = never, 1 = limited distance, 2 = always)

name: groupIndicators
description: Group indicators (0 = never, 1 = limited distance, 2 = always)

name: headlessClients
description: Headless Client IPs. Multiple connections and addresses are allowed in case of multiple Headless Clients. See Arma 3: Headless Client.

name: host
description: Start a non-dedicated multiplayer host.
since:
Start a non-dedicated multiplayer host.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -host

name: hostname
description: Servername visible in the game browser.

name: hugePages
description: Enables hugepages with the default memory allocator (malloc) for both client and server.

name: idleFPSLimit
description: Servers with no players will limit their FPS to the specified value. Supported range is 5-60 FPS

name: init
description: Run scripting command once in the main menu. For example to start a certain SP mission of choice automatically. See also playMission. The Mission has to reside in the "arma3\Missions" folder, NOT the user directory.
since:
Run scripting command once in the main menu. For example to start a certain SP mission of choice automatically. See also playMission. The Mission has to reside in the "arma3\Missions" folder, NOT the user directory.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -init=playMission["","Test.VR"]

name: ip
description: Command to enable support for Multihome servers. Allows server process to use defined available IP address. (b1.57.76934)
since:
Command to enable support for Multihome servers. Allows server process to use defined available IP address. (b1.57.76934)

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -ip=145.412.123.12

name: kickClientsOnSlowNetwork
description: Defines if {, , , } will be logged (0) or kicked (1)

name: kickduplicate
description: Do not allow duplicate game IDs. Second player with an existing ID will be kicked automatically. 1 means active, 0 disabled.

name: loadMissionToMemory
description: Server will load mission into memory on first client downloading it. Then it keeps it pre-processed pre-cached in memory for next clients, saving some server CPU cycles.
since:
Server will load mission into memory on first client downloading it. Then it keeps it pre-processed pre-cached in memory for next clients, saving some server CPU cycles.

; Example
 arma3server_x64.exe -loadMissionToMemory

name: localClient
description: Indicates clients with unlimited bandwidth and nearly no latency. See Arma 3: Headless Client.

name: logFile
description: Enables output of dedicated server console into textfile. Default location of log is same as crash dumps and other logs. (Local settings) Note that this does not change the location of the "net.log" file, which is enabled with the -netlog command line option.

name: loopback
description: Adding this option will force server into LAN mode. This will allow multiple local instances of the game to connect to the server for testing purposes. At the same time it will prevent all non-local instances from connecting.

name: malloc
description: Sets the particular memory allocator to be used. Significantly affects both performance and stability of the game. More details.
since:
Sets the particular memory allocator to be used. Significantly affects both performance and stability of the game. More details.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -malloc=someMalloc

name: mapContent
description: Extended map content

name: MaxBandwidth
description: Bandwidth the server is guaranteed to never have (in bps). This value helps the server to estimate bandwidth available.

name: MaxCustomFileSize
example: MaxCustomFileSize = 1024 || UNKNOWN || Users with custom face or custom sound larger than this size are kicked when trying to connect.

name: MaxDesync
description: Max desync value until server kick the user

name: maxFileCacheSize
description: Sets the default filecache size (when files are loaded from disk, they are cached in RAM. If the cache is full, the oldest file is thrown out).

name: maxMem
description: Overrides memory allocation limit to a certain amount (in megabytes).

name: MaxMsgSend
description: Maximum number of packets (aggregate messages) that can be sent in one simulation cycle ("frame"). Increasing this value can decrease lag on high upload bandwidth servers.

name: MaxPacketLoss
description: Max packetloss value until server kick the user

name: MaxPing
description: Max ping value until server kick the user

name: maxPlayers
description: The maximum number of players that can connect to server. The final number will be lesser between number given here and number of mission slots.

name: MaxSizeGuaranteed
description: Maximum size (payload) of guaranteed packet in bytes (without headers). Small messages are packed to larger packets (aggregate messages). Guaranteed packets (aggregate messages) are used for non-repetitive events like shooting.

name: MaxSizeNonguaranteed
description: Maximum size (payload) of non-guaranteed packet in bytes (without headers). Small messages are packed to larger packets (aggregate messages).  Non-guaranteed packets (aggregate messages) are used  for repetitive updates like soldier or vehicle position. Increasing this value may improve bandwidth requirement, but it may increase lag.

name: maxVRAM
description: Defines video memory allocation limit to number (in megabytes).
since:
Defines video memory allocation limit to number (in megabytes).

* Minimum value is 128 MiB (anything lower falls back to 128)
* The value is ignored (under DX11) if engine properly detected VRAM size, minus 20% reserve with ceiling limit 300MB max
* Use to resolve e.g. Windows problem: http://support.microsoft.com/kb/2026022/en-us?p=1

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -maxVRAM=4096

name: MinBandwidth
description: Bandwidth the server is guaranteed to have (in bps). This value helps server to estimate bandwidth available. Increasing it to too optimistic values can increase lag and CPU load, as too many messages will be sent but discarded.

name: MinErrorToSend
description: Minimal error to send updates across network. Using a smaller value can make units  observed by binoculars or sniper rifle to move smoother at the trade off of increased network traffic.

name: MinErrorToSendNear
description: Minimal error to send updates across network for near units. Using larger value can reduce traffic sent for near units. Used to control client to server traffic as well.

name: missionsToServerRestart
description: Number of times missionEnd happens before server initiate process restart (uses actual session startup command-line parameters, not possible to combine with `missionsToShutdown` )

name: missionsToShutdown
description: Number of times missionEnd happens before server initiate process shutdown (has same behavior as setting named `missionsToHardRestart`)

name: missionWhitelist
description: Limit the available missions for the admin for the mission change. See Arma 3: MP Mission Names for a full list.

name: motd
description: Two lines welcome message. Comma is the 'new line' separator.
example: motd[] = { "Welcome to my server.", "Hosted in the net." };

name: motdInterval
description: Time interval (in seconds) between each MOTD line.

name: multipleSaves
description: Multiple saves

name: name
description: Sets the profile name.
since:
Sets the profile name.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -name=PlayerOne

name: netlog
description: Enables multiplayer network traffic logging. For more details see server configuration.
since:
Enables multiplayer network traffic logging. For more details see server configuration.

; Example
 arma3server_x64.exe -netlog

name: noCB
description: Turns off multicore use. It slows down rendering but may resolve visual glitches.
since:
Turns off multicore use. It slows down rendering but may resolve visual glitches.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -noCB

name: noFilePatching
description: Ensures that only PBOs are loaded and NO unpacked data.

name: noFreezeCheck
description: Disables the freeze check. It creates otherwise max 4 dumps per game run in total - 2 per distinct freeze. Similar to Crash Files.
since:
Disables the freeze check. It creates otherwise max 4 dumps per game run in total - 2 per distinct freeze. Similar to Crash Files.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -noFreezeCheck

name: noLand
description: Starts with no world loaded. (Used for Buldozer)
since:
Starts with no world loaded. (Used for Buldozer)

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -noLand

name: noLogs
description: Be aware this means none errors saved to RPT file (report log). Yet in case of crash the fault address block info is saved.
since:
Be aware this means none errors saved to RPT file (report log). Yet in case of crash the fault address block info is saved.

More details .

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -noLogs

name: noPause
description: Allow the game running even when its window does not have focus (i.e. running in the background)

name: noSound
description: Disables sound output.
since:
Disables sound output.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -noSound

name: noSplash
description: Tells the engine to bypass the splash screens on startup of .

name: overrideHazeQuality
description: 0/1/2 - VeryLow/Low/Standard - Forces haze quality in MP on all clients. Standard (2) - mod config driven. Default (-1) - do not force. Mission config's  has priority over the server option.

name: par
description: Command to read startup parameters from a file. For more details see Startup parameters config file.
since:
Command to read startup parameters from a file. For more details see Startup parameters config file.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe "-par=C:\Users\Player\Games\Arma 3\Arma 3 Parameter Files\par_common.txt"

name: password
description: Server password to connect to.
since:
Server password to connect to.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -password=1337abc

name: passwordAdmin
description: Password to protect admin access.

name: persistent
description: Mission keeps running when all clients disconnect. Enabling the persistence option will make missions that have either base or instant respawn keep on running after all players have disconnected. The other respawn types will not make a mission persistent. The kind of respawn a certain mission uses is set in its Description.ext.

name: pid
description: File to write the server's PID (process ID) to.
since:
File to write the server's PID (process ID) to. 

* The file is removed automatically when the exe is stopped
* Only works for dedicated servers

; Example
 arma3server_x64.exe "-pid=C:\My Server\PID.txt"

name: port
description: Server port to connect to.
since:
Server port to connect to.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -port=1337

name: posX
description: Sets the default X Position of the Window.
since:
Sets the default X Position of the Window.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -posX=50

name: posY
description: Sets the default Y Position of the Window.
since:
Sets the default Y Position of the Window.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -posY=50

name: profiles
description: Location of user-profile folder.
since:
Location of user-profile folder.

If a custom path is set, default files and folders (logFiles, AnimDataCache, DataCache, SteamPreviewCache....) located by default in  will be created in that new location.

However, old files will stay in the old location and will not be copied.

; Example 1
 arma3_x64.exe -profiles=C:\arma3\Profiles

; Example 2
 arma3_x64.exe -profiles=Profiles

Example 2 create a folder called Profiles in the  root directory

name: randomMissionOrder
description: When enabled, the server random start / next selection with one of missions from mission rotation list. ( setting goes outside(before) Mission class {}; )

name: ranking
description: Allowing you to output ranking information or otherwise know as player stats to a file.

name: reducedDamage
description: Reduced damage

name: requiredBuild
description: Minimum required client version. Clients with version lower than requiredBuild will not be able to connect. If requiredBuild is set to a large number, like requiredBuild = 999999999; for example, it will automatically be lowered to the current server version.

name: roleTimeOut
description: Role selection timout

name: scoreTable
description: Score table

name: server
description: Start a dedicated server. Not needed for the dedicated server exe.
since:
Start a dedicated server. Not needed for the dedicated server exe.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -server

name: serverCommandPassword
description: Password required by alternate syntax of serverCommand server-side scripting (Case-Sensitive).

name: serverMod
description: Loads the specified sub-folders for different server-side (not broadcasted to clients) mods.

name: setUpHost
description: Start the game in the setup server display. Works with the client exe. See reference image
since:
Start the game in the setup server display. Works with the client exe. See reference image

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -setUpHost

name: showScriptErrors
description: Introduced to show errors in scripts on-screen. In Eden Editor, script errors are always shown, even when this parameter is not used.
since:
Introduced to show errors in scripts on-screen. In Eden Editor, script errors are always shown, even when this parameter is not used.

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -showScriptErrors

name: skipIntro
description: Disables world intros in the main menu permanently.

name: skipLobby
description: If true, joining player will skip role selection. This is only used if no Mission, Campaign or Config setting skipLobby is defined (See Description.ext#skipLobby).

name: staminaBar
description: Stamina bar

name: stanceIndicator
description: Stance indicator (0 = never, 1 = fade out, 2 = always)

name: statisticsEnabled
description: Allows to opt-out of Arma 3 analytics for the server by using 0

name: steamProtocolMaxDataSize
description: Limit for maximum Steam Query packet length. Increasing this value is dangerous as it can cause  server to send UDP packets of a size larger than the MTU. This will cause UDP packets to be fragmented which is not supported by some older routers. But increasing this will fix the modlist length limit in Arma 3: Launcher.

name: tacticalPing
description: Tactical ping (0 = disable, 1 = enable)

name: thirdPersonView
description: 3rd person view (0 = disabled, 1 = enabled, 2 = enabled for vehicles only (Since  Arma 3 v1.99))

name: timeStampFormat
description: Set the timestamp format used on each report line in server-side RPT file. Possible values are "none", "short", "full".

name: timeStampFormatConsole
description: Set the timestamp format used on each report line in server console. Possible values are "none", "short", "full".

name: unit
description: Parameter passes a unit's ID number to the binary, translates to https://units.arma3.com/my-units#?unit=
since:
Parameter passes a unit's ID number to the binary, translates to https://units.arma3.com/my-units#?unit=

; Example
 arma3_x64.exe -unit=1337

name: upnp
description: Automatically creates port mapping on UPNP/IGD enabled router. This option allows to create a server behind NAT (the router must have public IP and support UPNP/IGD protocol). Read more .

name: verifySignatures
description: Enables or disables the signature verification for addons.

name: visionAid
description: Vision aid

name: vonID
description: VoN ID

name: voteMissionPlayers
description: Start mission-voting when X numberOfPlayers connect.

name: voteThreshold
description: Percentage of votes needed to confirm a vote. 33% in this example.

name: votingTimeOut
description: Voting timeout

name: waypoints
description: Waypoints (0 = never, 1 = fade out, 2 = always)

name: weaponCrosshair
description: Weapon crosshair

name: weaponInfo
description: Weapon info (0 = never, 1 = fade out, 2 = always)

name: window
description: Displays  windowed instead of full screen. Screen resolution / window size are set in arma3.cfg.

name: world
description: Select a world loaded by default. For faster game loading (no default world loaded and world intro in the main menu, only at game start, disabled): -world=empty.
since:
Select a world loaded by default. For faster game loading (no default world loaded and world intro in the main menu, only at game start, disabled): -world=empty.

; Example
For faster game loading (no default world loaded and world intro in the main menu, only at game start, disabled)
 arma3_x64.exe -world=empty
Load Altis
 arma3_x64.exe -world=Altis

name: worldCfg
description: Inits a landscape by the given world config.
since: Inits a landscape by the given world config.
